content.full_text:
September 14, 1976


Mr. Bernard J. Favaro
Favaro & Lavezzo
A Professional Corporation
Courthouse Plaza, Suite A
300 Tuolumne Street
Vallejo, CA 94580

Dear Mr. Favaro:

This letter is to confirm our telephone conversa-
tion of September 8, and to respond to your letter to the
Commission dated August 25.

Government Code Section 82046 defines "public offi-
cial" as "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a
state or local government agency." Section 82019 which de-
fines "designated employee" uses similar language. Special
districts of the type you represent are local government
agencies, and special counsel for such agencies may generally
be said to be "consultants" of the agency. However, in defin-
ing "consultant" the Commission has adopted regulation 2
Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(a)(2) which provides:

"Consultant" shall include any natural person
who provides, under contract, information, advice,
recommendation or counsel to a state or local gov-
ernment agency, provided, however, that "consultant"
shall not include a person who:

(A) Conducts research and arrives at
conclusions with respect to his or her rendi-
tion of information, advice, recommendation
or counsel independent of the control and
direction of the agency or of any agency of-
ficial, other than normal contract monitoring;
and

(B) Possesses no authority with respect
to any agency decision beyond the rendition of
information, advice, recommendation or counsel.

The effect of this regulation will be to remove
many "independent contractors" from the scope of coverage of

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with only some very faint markings or artifacts visible in the upper right corner. The majority of the page is white/empty space. Without clear, legible text to transcribe, I cannot provide a meaningful transcription following the rules specified.

Mr. Bernard J. Pavero
September 14, 1976
Page Two


the Political Reform Act. Such would appear to be the case
in your situation. I base this conclusion on the fact that
you advise the Districts (or the Engineer-Manager) on an ad-
hoc basis; have no real or apparent authority to bind the
Districts to any course of action; and that your role is that
of one solely rendering "information, advice, recommendation,
or counsel." In light of this conclusion, it would not appear
necessary to reach the question of whether your advice is
subject to "significant intervening substantive review."

Thank you for writing. I trust this answers your
question.

Sincerely,


Kenneth W. Coshorn
Research Specialist III
Conflicts of Interest
Division

KWGzplh

LAW OFFICES

FAVARO & LAVEZZO
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

BERNARD J. FAVARO
ALBERT M. LAVEZZO

THOMAS L. GILL

COURTHOUSE PLAZA, SUITE A
300 TUOLUMNE STREET

VALLEJO, CALIFORNIA 94590

(707) 552-3630

OF COUNSEL

SCHWARTZ & LANE, INC.
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA

August 25, 1976

Executive Director,
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sir:

     This office represents two special districts (a recreation
district and a sanitation district).  In the preparation of
conflict of interest codes we have included ourselves as
designated employees whose positions should be included in the
codes.

     We did so because it would seem that by definition we do
"influence governmental decisions" in which we might have a
financial interest.

     It is also clear, however, that in every case in which we
advise the decision-maker there is a "significant intervening
substantive review".

     I've read your latest Bulletin (August 20, Vol. 2, No. 8).
I would appreciate your comment on the clarification set forth
therein with respect to our position. We are not objecting to
the inclusion, you understand; we just don't want to clutter up
the codes unnecessarily. Can you tell me whether attorneys
representing such districts elsewhere in the State are so included
generally?

     May I hear from you at your earliest convenience.

                                             Very truly yours,

                                             BERNARD J. FAVARO


BJF:ms

[The image shows a heavily faded and illegible document. Most of the text is too unclear to read with confidence. There appear to be some stamps or markings on the left side of the page, and various lines of text throughout, but the specific content cannot be reliably transcribed due to the poor quality and fading of the document.]
